headline: The enterprise shift OpenAI saw coming
subtitle: Anthropic seizes business AI lead · Amazon consolidates Alexa shopping · Claude Code automation tools
intro: Anthropic has overtaken OpenAI in enterprise adoption for the first time, according to new spending data from fintech platform Ramp. The shift reflects OpenAI's strategic pivot toward enterprise-focused features throughout 2026, following internal concerns about losing market share to competitors.

bullets.text: Anthropic leads enterprise AI adoption, overtaking OpenAI for first time
bullets.summary: Ramp's latest AI Index shows Anthropic at 34.4% of paid business adoption versus OpenAI's 32.3%, with Anthropic surging 4x since 2025. Claude Code's expansion into finance, legal, and research workflows drove much of the gain, though Ramp notes risks including recent outages and rising costs.

bullets.text: Amazon consolidates Rufus into Alexa for Shopping agent
bullets.summary: Amazon folded its standalone Rufus chatbot into a new Alexa for Shopping agent that tracks purchases and preferences across devices. The consolidated platform adds features like price tracking, Auto-Buy, and cross-store checkout, building on Rufus's 300M+ user base from 2025.

bullets.text: Adaption's AutoScientist automates custom AI model training
bullets.summary: AutoScientist automatically customizes AI models by optimizing training data and learning approaches. In tests across multiple models and industries, it outperformed expert-tuned versions by 35% and boosted success rates from 48% to 64%.

bullets.text: Claude Code and Higgsfield enable multi-model image generation
bullets.summary: A new workflow connects Higgsfield CLI to Claude Code, allowing users to send single prompts across multiple image models simultaneously and compare results. The guide walks through setup, model selection, and iterative refinement.

bullets.text: Google Cloud launches Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
bullets.summary: Google Cloud released an Agent Development Kit for building and orchestrating specialized agents with self-correcting feedback loops. The codelab teaches deployment to production on Google Cloud Run.

bullets.text: AI chipmaker Cerebras raises $5.55B in record IPO
bullets.summary: Cerebras completed the year's biggest IPO by raising $5.55 billion, marking a major milestone for AI infrastructure companies. The IPO validates growing demand for specialized AI chips as enterprises scale their AI operations.

bullets.text: Claude available for small business use cases
bullets.summary: Anthropic unveiled Claude tailored specifically for small business needs, making advanced AI capabilities accessible to SMBs. The offering includes pricing and features designed to meet smaller companies' budget and complexity constraints.

bullets.text: Major researchers secure $4B for self-improving AI
bullets.summary: A $4 billion funding round brought together notable AI researchers to advance self-improving systems. The initiative represents significant investment in developing AI that can autonomously enhance its own capabilities.

bullets.text: OpenAI details Codex Windows Sandbox architecture
bullets.summary: OpenAI published an in-depth technical overview of how it built the Codex Windows Sandbox. The architecture enables safe execution of AI-generated code in isolated environments, a critical safety consideration for code generation systems.

bullets.text: Vercel tracks AI Gateway production usage patterns
bullets.summary: Vercel released production index data tracking how developers use AI gateways across applications. The data provides insights into emerging patterns in AI service adoption and integration into real-world applications.

bullets.text: Microsoft's multi-agent system wins cybersecurity benchmark
bullets.summary: Microsoft's multi-agent AI approach outperformed Anthropic's Mythos on a cybersecurity benchmark. The result highlights the competitive advantages of coordinated agent systems for complex security tasks.

bullets.text: Claude pricing shifting for third-party tool usage
bullets.summary: Claude is introducing separate limits for third-party tool usage with credits equal to plan value. After June 15th, usage beyond credits charges at API rates, though the team is raising weekly limits by 50% for two months as transition help.

bullets.text: Google launches Gemini Intelligence features on Android
bullets.summary: Google released Gemini Intelligence with form auto-completion, voice note transcription, and app automations. They also introduced a new laptop category called Googlebooks before their I/O conference.

bullets.text: Notion unveils developer platform with markdown API
bullets.summary: Notion's new developer platform enables markdown APIs, external data syncing, custom tools for Notion Agents, and infrastructure-level code execution. A CLI called ntn accompanies the launch, opening possibilities for AI agents like Claude as workspace teammates.

bullets.text: Vercel's AI Gateway index shows agentic workloads dominate
bullets.summary: Vercel published production usage data showing Anthropic leads spend (61%), Google leads token volume (38%), and agentic workloads represent 59% of all token usage. Most large teams route across multiple models rather than betting on one.

bullets.text: Cursor enables cloud agents in configured dev environments
bullets.summary: Cursor launched support for running cloud agents inside fully configured development environments. Orca provides Claude Code's agent view adapted for other models like Codex and OpenCode.

bullets.text: Intercom rebranding entirely to Fin, their AI agent
bullets.summary: The company behind Intercom is undergoing a complete rebrand centered on Fin, their popular AI agent product, signaling a strategic shift toward agent-first positioning.

bullets.text: Anthropic clinches #1 business AI ranking with 34.4% adoption
bullets.summary: Ramp's latest spending data shows Anthropic crossing OpenAI for the first time in enterprise adoption. Claude Code, the autonomous coding tool, generated $2.5B in annualized revenue by February 2026 alone, driving the reversal from a position where OpenAI led 32% to 8% just a year earlier.

bullets.text: Palo Alto warns AI-powered cyberattacks becoming standard
bullets.summary: Palo Alto Networks flagged that frontier AI models like Anthropic's Mythos and OpenAI's GPT-5.5-Cyber are enabling more sophisticated attacks. The UK's AI Safety Institute separately found autonomous AI cyber capabilities doubling roughly every 4.7 months.

bullets.text: Apple building AI agents into App Store ecosystem
bullets.summary: Apple is reportedly developing AI agent support directly within the App Store, with an announcement expected at WWDC. This would allow users to discover, download, and run AI agents alongside traditional apps on iPhones.

bullets.text: Notion launches developer platform for AI agent integration
bullets.summary: Notion released v3.5 of its developer platform enabling teams to sync data sources and build custom agent tools within their workspace. Users can invite Claude, Codex, or custom agents directly as collaborators to handle tasks.

bullets.text: Google DeepMind introduces context-aware AI Pointer cursor
bullets.summary: Google DeepMind unveiled AI Pointer, transforming the traditional mouse cursor into an intelligent collaborator that understands screen context and can act on it. The concept is launching initially in Chrome.

main_story.title: Anthropic seizes OpenAI's enterprise AI lead
main_story.body: Ramp's latest AI Index reveals a dramatic shift in enterprise adoption patterns. Anthropic has surpassed OpenAI for the first time among paid business users, capturing 34.4% adoption versus OpenAI's 32.3% in May 2026. This represents a 4x surge in Anthropic adoption since 2025, while OpenAI's usage has plateaued. The momentum appears driven by Claude Code's successful expansion beyond technical teams into finance, legal, and research workflows. Ramp's data comes from tracking corporate spending across 50,000+ U.S. businesses, providing a reliable spend signal even if it doesn't capture all enterprise deals.
main_story.why: This marks the realization of concerns OpenAI leadership raised two months ago when CEO Fidji Simo declared a code red over Anthropic's enterprise momentum. The shift validates the strategic pivot OpenAI has pursued throughout 2026, including recent enterprise-focused features and initiatives.
main_story.take: While the lead remains narrow and OpenAI retains advantages in consumer adoption and untracked large enterprise contracts, the yearly trend signals a meaningful competitive shift in enterprise AI. OpenAI's response reflects how quickly market dynamics can change in the AI race.

Sam Altman testifies that Musk's safety concerns prompted idea to pass OpenAI to his children
Microsoft's MDASH chains 100+ agents to hunt software bugs, catching 16 Windows flaws
UK AI Safety Institute warns cyberattack capability doubles every few months

previous.headline: Android enters its Gemini Intelligence era
previous.subtitle: Google's AI-native Googlebooks · SpaceX orbital compute · Claude Code financial analyst
previous.intro: Google is rolling out major Gemini integrations across Android devices ahead of next week's I/O event, including new AI-native Googlebook laptops with a Magic Pointer cursor and cross-device intelligence system. The company is also exploring orbital data centers with SpaceX while managing internal token-gaming issues at Amazon.

bullets.text: Google launches Googlebooks and Gemini Intelligence system
bullets.summary: Google introduced AI-native Googlebook laptops built with major manufacturers, featuring a Magic Pointer AI cursor and Gemini Intelligence as Android's cross-device AI platform. The new system handles agentic tasks within apps using on-screen context, alongside other releases like a Create My Widget tool and Rambler dictation feature.

bullets.text: Google explores SpaceX partnership for orbital data centers
bullets.summary: Google is reportedly negotiating with SpaceX to launch orbital data centers, leveraging its existing 6.1% stake in the company and board seat. This complements Google's Project Suncatcher satellite initiative and follows Anthropic's recent orbital compute deal with SpaceX.

bullets.text: Transform Claude Code into financial research and analysis tool
bullets.summary: Users can add Anthropic's financial services plugin marketplace to Claude Code to access market research, equity analysis, and earnings review capabilities. The guide walks through installing market-researcher, earnings-reviewer, and equity-research plugins for creating sourced financial reports.

bullets.text: Amazon's AI incentives create problematic token-usage gaming
bullets.summary: Amazon employees are gaming the MeshClaw AI tool to burn unnecessary tokens and boost adoption metrics, creating perverse workplace incentives. The company has backpedaled on token visibility after recognizing that rewarding usage volume rather than actual productivity improvements distorts employee behavior.

bullets.text: Google's Isomorphic Labs secures $2.1B for drug discovery AI
bullets.summary: Google's drug discovery subsidiary Isomorphic Labs announced a major funding round with leadership emphasizing that improving human health represents AI's most important application. The investment underscores growing confidence in AI-driven pharmaceutical development.

bullets.text: Security breach plants malware in open-source AI tool packages
bullets.summary: A hacker inserted data-stealing code called Mini Shai-Hulud into 42 npm packages used by AI tools, marking a widespread supply-chain attack. The incident affected multiple AI applications relying on open-source dependencies.

bullets.text: Claude Opus 4.7 launches fast mode for quicker inference
bullets.summary: Anthropic introduces a faster variant of Claude Opus 4.7, prioritizing response speed for latency-sensitive applications. This addresses developer demand for quicker inference without sacrificing capability.

bullets.text: Qwen-Image-2.0 advances multimodal AI with new capabilities
bullets.summary: Alibaba's Qwen releases its latest image understanding and generation model with improved performance metrics. The technical report details architectural improvements enabling stronger visual reasoning.

bullets.text: Truly serverless GPUs reshape cloud compute architecture
bullets.summary: Modal explores practical approaches to serverless GPU infrastructure, eliminating idle capacity costs. The article outlines technical patterns for on-demand GPU provisioning in production environments.

bullets.text: Meta Glasses integration with Muse Spark voice assistant
bullets.summary: Meta plans to integrate Muse Spark voice capabilities directly into its glasses hardware. This convergence of AI assistants and wearable devices signals broader adoption of always-on AI interfaces.

bullets.text: Perceptron Mk1 delivers cost-efficient video analysis model
bullets.summary: A new video analysis model achieves comparable performance to major providers at fraction of the cost. The efficiency breakthrough suggests emerging competition in specialized AI workloads.

bullets.text: Claude Code agents now work together in single terminal window
bullets.summary: Users can now manage multiple Claude Code agents in one terminal interface, viewing their status and replying inline to unblock when agents need input. Running sessions can be moved to agent view with the /bg command.

bullets.text: OpenAI releases three new Realtime API models for audio
bullets.summary: OpenAI launched Realtime 2 for voice-to-voice interactions, Realtime Translate supporting 70 input and 13 output languages, and Realtime-Whisper for live speech-to-text conversion.

bullets.text: OpenAI launches $4B deployment company with consulting firms
bullets.summary: OpenAI acquired Tomoro, a 150-person AI consulting company, and invested $4 billion to create a deployment company partnering with major consultants. The goal is helping enterprises build custom AI systems and upskill knowledge workers to become builders.

bullets.text: OpenAI releases Daybreak cyber defense product
bullets.summary: OpenAI launched Daybreak as its answer to Anthropic's cybersecurity offerings, positioning it as a defense-focused product for enterprise security needs.

bullets.text: Thinking Machines shows interaction models with multimodal capabilities
bullets.summary: Thinking Machines unveiled interaction models supporting chat with audio and video input plus audio output, claiming time awareness and simultaneous speech-visual processing. However, similar products from competitors have struggled in real-world use.

bullets.text: Codex Chrome extension enables cross-tab background browsing
bullets.summary: Codex now works natively in Chrome on macOS and Windows, allowing it to use multiple sites and apps across tabs in the background without taking over the browser.

bullets.text: Google's Magic Pointer uses AI to understand cursor intent
bullets.summary: Google DeepMind unveiled Magic Pointer, a Gemini-powered cursor that interprets what users are pointing at rather than just registering clicks. It can automatically create meetings from dates, convert tables into charts, and find restaurants from paused travel videos without requiring explicit prompts.

bullets.text: First pilotable mech suit becomes operational reality
bullets.summary: A massive, pilotable mech suit inspired by Japanese anime has been built and demonstrated. The suit can knock over walls and transform into a quadruped dog form, representing a major milestone in robotics engineering.

main_story.title: Google's unified Gemini approach marks shift in AI device integration
main_story.body: Google is taking a different approach to AI adoption than competitors by weaving Gemini directly into Android rather than bolting it on as a separate feature. The company unveiled AI-native Googlebook laptops featuring a Magic Pointer cursor interface, along with Gemini Intelligence as a cross-device platform capable of executing agentic tasks with on-screen context awareness. This unified strategy precedes Google's full I/O presentation next week and includes additional tools like Create My Widget and Rambler voice dictation. The integration positions Android as a cohesive AI ecosystem blending ChromeOS, Android, Google Play, and Gemini capabilities.
main_story.why: While Apple works on reviving Siri and other tech giants treat AI as an add-on, Google's system-level integration could actually make AI useful by providing context-aware intelligence across devices. This approach addresses a core challenge in AI adoption: most users don't yet understand how AI improves their daily workflows.
main_story.take: Google may be the first company to successfully crack meaningful device-level AI integration, transforming the AI-native concept from marketing phrase to actual user experience. If executed well, this could set the standard for how AI should interact with consumer hardware going forward.

Meta employees protest mouse-tracking software used for AI training
Rivian rolls out AI assistant controlling EV hardware via voice or button
